rationale: The US-Iran conflict with Strait of Hormuz closure continues at the same intensity as the previous cycle — no qualitative escalation detected, so the baseline holds but does not rise; oil supply disruption risk remains the primary indirect threat to Polish energy prices.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue, Strait of Hormuz closed
top_events.summary: Al Jazeera reports day 104 of the Iran war, with Iran attacking US bases and closing the strait after Trump strikes; US released video of warship firing missiles. No qualitative change from previous cycle — the closure and mutual strikes were already known — but the sustained disruption to oil supply keeps indirect pressure on Polish fuel and energy prices elevated.

top_events.title: UN watchdog demands Iran nuclear stockpile information
top_events.summary: The IAEA has formally demanded Iran provide information on its nuclear stockpile, adding a new diplomatic dimension to the ongoing US-Iran conflict. If Iran's nuclear program becomes a direct flashpoint, escalation risk for Europe and NATO increases, though this remains a procedural step for now.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: China's president arrived in North Korea to strengthen bilateral ties, a geopolitical signal that could affect the broader NATO-Russia-China strategic balance. Indirect relevance for Poland as a NATO member, but no immediate operational change.
